data.title: ATOM Rebounds After Dip, Establishes New Support Level
data.description:
• ATOM cryptocurrency shows resilience, establishing new support at $3.994-$4.000 despite recent market volatility influenced by conflict in the Middle East.
  • The overall range of $0.121 (3.05%) reflects moderate volatility, with a notable bullish surge occurring at 20:00 when price jumped 2.16% on above-average volume, establishing resistance at $4.059.

data.title: Why Pro-Israel Group's $90M Crypto Hack Could Be a Hammer Blow for Iran's Regime
data.description:
• Nobitex was hacked on Wednesday, with attackers stealing $90 million and prompting a full service shutdown.
  • Pro-Israel group Gonjeshke Darande claimed responsibility, using "vanity" wallets with messages like “terrorist” to burn the funds and send a political message, not profit.
  • The hack potentially cripples Iran’s ability to move funds via crypto amid heightened Middle East tensions, exposing vulnerabilities in its sanction-evasion infrastructure.

data.title: ADA Slips Below $0.60; 24-Hour Trading Volume Jumps 30% Amid Accumulation Signs
data.description:
• ADA dropped to $0.5965, falling below the $0.60 psychological level despite early support formation.
  • 24-hour trading volume spiked 30% above the 7-day average, suggesting elevated market interest.
  • Market structure shows potential accumulation with reduced selling pressure and higher lows forming intraday.

data.title: Visa Expands Stablecoin Reach in Europe, Middle East and Africa
data.description:
• In 2025, we believe that every institution that moves money will need a stablecoin strategy, Visa said.
  • The Yellow Card partnership will explore cross-border payment options, streamlining treasury operations and enhancing liquidity management.

data.title: Bitcoin Cash (BCH) Jumps to $480 as Trading Volume Spikes 22% Above Average
data.description:
• BCH rose 3.15% to $480.10, rebounding from $454 support after a wave of buying pressure broke through the $465 level.
  • 24-hour trading volume surged 21.98% above the 30-day average, helping fuel a late-session breakout to session highs.
  • Notable volume spikes during the New York morning session and final hour confirmed strong buyer interest at higher levels.

data.title: Crypto Exchange Kraken Adds Bitcoin Staking Via Babylon as BTC Driven DeFi Picks Up
data.description:
• Kraken has launched a bitcoin staking service.
  • The new offering allows Kraken customers to stake their bitcoin while locking their crypto in a custodial vault on the native chain.

data.title: Nobitex's Source Code Released a Day After Hackers Steal Tokens Across Bitcoin, EVM, Ripple Networks
data.description:
• The pro-Israel hacker group Gonjeshke Darande released the full source code of Iranian crypto exchange Nobitex after conducting a $100 million exploit.
  • The leak exposes users' assets to potential theft because the public code dismantles the platform's back-end security.
  • Nobitex said it plans to restore services within five days despite internet disruptions in Iran.

data.title: Return of Zero Interest Rate Policy as Swiss Central Bank Cuts Rates
data.description:
• Switzerland's central bank slashed interest rates to zero, its sixth cut since March 2024.
  • The move counters deflation and currency pressure from Trump’s trade war.
  • A return to zero interest rates globally could boost bitcoin and other crypto, as seen during the COVID-era rally.

data.title: Circle Rockets After Stablecoin Bill Clears Senate, Pushes Post-IPO Rally to Over 500%
data.description:
• Circle shares surged 34% after the U.S. Senate passed a bill supporting regulated stablecoins, with further gains in after-hours trading.
  • The GENIUS Act, which still requires House approval and the President's signature, aims to integrate stablecoins into the U.S. financial system.
  • Circle's stock has risen over 540% since its debut, reflecting investor optimism about its role in the future of digital payments.

data.title: KuCoin Allows Institutional Clients to Trade Without Having to Pre-Fund Wallets
data.description:
• KuCoin is now allowing institutional clients to trade without having to pre-fund wallets.
  • Institutional clients' assets remain in BitGo Singapore's storage, following the model of separating custody and execution to mitigate counterparty and systemic risk.
  • KuCoin is attempting to offering institutional clients a trading experience that they would recognize TradFi world.
